headlines: Israeli–Syrian ceasefire line incidents during the Syrian civil war
Prime minister of New Zealand Jacinda Ardern announces the deployment of 120 New Zealand troops to the United Kingdom to train Ukrainian forces.

events.text: Operation Barkhane

France formally ends its military intervention in Mali after withdrawing its last remaining troops from a military base in Gao. The base was handed over to Malian Armed Forces before the French forces crossed into neighbouring Niger.
Four Russian Wagner Group paramilitaries are killed during an ambush by Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin militants in Bandiagara.
War in Afghanistan
Fall of Kabul
The Taliban stages a Victory Day parade on the first anniversary of the capture of Kabul.
Israeli–Syrian ceasefire line incidents during the Syrian civil war
Israel launches a series of airstrikes on Damascus and Tartus, Syria, killing three soldiers and injuring three others.
COVID-19 pandemic
COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom
COVID-19 vaccination in the United Kingdom
The United Kingdom approves a Moderna COVID-19 vaccine aimed at targeting the Omicron variant and other variants of SARS-CoV-2, becoming the first country to do so.
Liberia–United States relations
The United States imposes sanctions on three senior members of the cabinet of Liberian president George Weah, accusing them of corruption.
Prime minister of New Zealand Jacinda Ardern announces the deployment of 120 New Zealand troops to the United Kingdom to train Ukrainian forces.
2021 Myanmar coup d'état
The military junta court sentences former state counsellor Aung San Suu Kyi to six years in prison for corruption.
A lawsuit is filed against the Central Intelligence Agency and its former director Mike Pompeo, accusing the agency of spying on lawyers and journalists who met with Julian Assange.
2022 Kenyan general election
The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission declares William Ruto as the winner of the election.

FIFA suspends the All India Football Federation for violating its statutes on third-party interference. India is stripped of its hosting rights for international football tournaments, including the 2022 FIFA U-17 Women's World Cup scheduled for October 2022.
